rdf:Description.dc:publisher: Die Schriftkultur des christlichen Äthiopiens und Eritreas: Eine multimediale Forschungsumgebung / Beta maṣāḥǝft,

rdf:Description.dcterms:hasPart: Homily by Dämatewos, Archbishop of Alexandria
Homily by John, the archbishop of Antioch
Homily on the greatness or glory of the Archangel Michael
Homily by Yoḥannǝs, Bishop of Ethiopia, who came after Bishop Yǝṣḥaq, on the conflict between the consort of King Arqadewos and John Chysostom
Homily on the family of Astäraniqos and Euphemia
Homily (by Bishop Yoḥannǝs) on the wicked wealthy man whose property was inherited by the son of his poor neighbor
Homily (anonymous) on the book of the angels that came from Jerusalem
Homily (anonymous) on not worshipping other gods
Homily (anonymous) on the need to make the angels sureties by honoring them
Synaxary entry: Mission of the Archangel to Joshua, son of Nun
Synaxary Entry: Mission of the Archangel to rescue the three holy children
Synaxary Entry: Mission of the Archangel to save Jacob from the wrath of Esau
Synaxary Entry: Mission of the Archangel to Samson, the giant
Synaxary Entry: Mission of the Archangel to Balaam
Note: “For Miyazya and Gǝnbot, it is written at the end of the book.”
Synaxary entry: The building of the church of the Archangel in place of a pagan temple in Alexandria
Synaxary entry about the Elevation of Saint Michael, with greeting
Synaxary entry about the death and remembrance of Afomǝya, with greeting
Synaxary entry about the death and remembrance of Lalibäla, king of Ethiopia, with greeting
Synaxary entry: Mission to the camp of Sennacherib, with greeting
Synaxary entry: Mission of the Archangel to the Emperor Constantine
: Two Greetings, one to the Archangel, another to Constantine
Synaxary entry: Mission to Prophet Isaiah
Synaxary entry: Mission to Prophet Samuel
Synaxary entry: Mission to David to help him with power to kill Goliath
Two Greetings to the Archangel, Sälam
Synaxary entries: Mission of the Archangel to the Prophet Jeremiah
